name: mysql-mcp
description: A Model Context Protocol for MySQL database interaction

tools.name: list_databases
tools.description: Lists all databases available on the MySQL server. Use this to discover what databases exist before querying them.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: Lists all tables in the connected database. Use this as the first step when exploring an unfamiliar database to see available tables.

database.description: Optional: specific database name to list tables from

tools.name: get_database_summary
tools.description: 📊 Returns a high-level overview of the database including all tables, their columns, data types, row counts, and relationships. RECOMMENDED: Use this first when exploring a new database to understand its structure quickly. Features: database overview, per-table breakdown with primary keys, columns with nullable info and foreign key references, optional relationship summary, and configurable table limits.

max_tables.description: Optional: maximum number of tables to include (default: all tables, max 500)

include_relationships.description: Optional: include foreign key relationships section (default: true)

tools.name: get_schema_erd
tools.description: 📈 Generates a visual Mermaid.js ER diagram showing tables and their relationships. Perfect for visualizing database structure and foreign key connections. Use when users ask to 'visualize' or 'diagram' the database.

tools.name: get_schema_rag_context
tools.description: 🎯 AI-OPTIMIZED: Returns ultra-compact schema information (tables, columns, keys, relationships, row estimates) designed specifically for LLM context windows. Use this when you need schema awareness but want to minimize token usage. Configurable limits for tables/columns.

max_tables.description: Optional: maximum number of tables to include (default 50, max 200)

max_columns.description: Optional: maximum number of columns per table (default 12, max 200)

include_relationships.description: Whether to include FK relationships section (default: true)

tools.name: get_column_statistics
tools.description: Returns detailed statistics for a specific column: min/max values, average, distinct count, null percentage, and value distribution. Use to understand data quality and ranges in a column before writing queries.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: read_table_schema
tools.description: Returns complete schema definition for a table: columns, data types, constraints, keys, indexes, and defaults. Use when you need detailed structural information about a specific table.

table_name.description: Name of the table to read schema from

tools.name: create_record
tools.description: Inserts a single new record into a table. For inserting multiple records at once, use bulk_insert for better performance.

table_name.description: Name of the table to insert into

data.description: Object containing column names and values to insert

tools.name: read_records
tools.description: Reads records from a table with built-in filtering, pagination, and sorting. Use this for simple data retrieval. For complex queries with JOINs or aggregations, use run_select_query instead.

table_name.description: Name of the table to read from

value.description: Value to compare against (can be string, number, boolean, or array for "in" operator)

tools.name: update_record
tools.description: Updates records in a table based on specified conditions. For updating many records with different values, use bulk_update for better performance.

table_name.description: Name of the table to update

data.description: Object containing column names and new values

conditions.description: Array of conditions to identify which records to update

tools.name: delete_record
tools.description: Deletes records from a table based on specified conditions. Always requires conditions for safety (no WHERE-less deletes). For deleting multiple record sets, use bulk_delete.

table_name.description: Name of the table to delete from

conditions.description: Array of conditions to identify which records to delete

tools.name: bulk_insert
tools.description: ⚡ PERFORMANCE: Inserts multiple records efficiently using batch processing. Handles 1000s of rows with automatic batching. Use this instead of create_record when inserting many records at once (10+ rows).

table_name.description: Name of the table to insert into

data.description: Array of objects containing column names and values to insert

batch_size.description: Optional batch size for processing (default: 1000, max: 10000)

tools.name: bulk_update
tools.description: ⚡ PERFORMANCE: Updates multiple records with different values in batches. Each update can have unique conditions and data. Much faster than calling update_record repeatedly for multiple rows.

table_name.description: Name of the table to update

updates.description: Array of update operations with data and conditions

data.description: Object containing column names and new values

conditions.description: Array of conditions to identify which records to update

batch_size.description: Optional batch size for processing (default: 100, max: 1000)

tools.name: bulk_delete
tools.description: ⚡ PERFORMANCE: Deletes multiple sets of records efficiently in batches. Each condition set defines a separate delete operation. More efficient than calling delete_record multiple times.

table_name.description: Name of the table to delete from

condition_sets.description: Array of condition sets, each defining records to delete

items.description: Array of conditions for this delete operation

batch_size.description: Optional batch size for processing (default: 100, max: 1000)

tools.name: plan_seed_data
tools.description: 🌱 RELATIONAL SEEDER: Analyzes target tables, foreign keys, constraints, and row counts to build a safe parent-first seed plan. Use this before generating preview or inserting dummy relational data.

rows_per_table.description: Rows to create for target tables, either a single number or table-to-count map (default: 10)

include_dependencies.description: Include parent tables required by foreign keys (default: true)

include_children.description: Include child tables that reference included tables for nested relational seeding (default: false)

child_rows_per_parent.description: Rows to create in child tables for each parent row (default: 2)

respect_existing_data.description: Reuse existing parent records when possible instead of creating new parent rows (default: true)

random_seed.description: Deterministic seed for repeatable preview and execution (default: 42)

max_rows_per_table.description: Safety cap for generated rows per table (default: 1000)

seed_rules.description: Optional generator overrides keyed by table.column or column name

require_confirmation.description: Require confirm_token for non-dry-run execution (default: true)

tools.name: generate_seed_preview
tools.description: 🌱 RELATIONAL SEEDER: Generates deterministic dummy row previews from a seed plan without writing to the database. Shows symbolic foreign-key placeholders for review before execution.

plan_id.description: Seed plan ID returned by plan_seed_data

locale.description: Optional locale hint for generated values (default: en_US)

realistic.description: Whether to prefer realistic generated values (default: true)

email_domain.description: Safe email domain for generated emails (default: example.test)

tools.name: execute_seed_plan
tools.description: 🌱 RELATIONAL SEEDER: Executes a confirmed seed plan with dry-run enabled by default, production-name guard, transaction rollback on errors, and foreign-key ID resolution.

plan_id.description: Seed plan ID returned by plan_seed_data

dry_run.description: If true, returns execution preview without inserting rows (default: true)

use_transaction.description: Use a transaction and rollback on error (default: true)

batch_size.description: Reserved batch-size hint for large plans (default: 1 for ID-safe inserts)

confirm_token.description: Confirmation token returned by plan_seed_data; required when dry_run is false

allow_production.description: Allow writes to production-like database names (default: false)

email_domain.description: Safe email domain for generated emails (default: example.test)

tools.name: validate_seed_integrity
tools.description: 🌱 RELATIONAL SEEDER: Validates seed results with FK orphan checks, required-column checks, unique-collision checks, and inserted row-count checks.

plan_id.description: Seed plan ID returned by plan_seed_data

check_required_columns.description: Check NOT NULL required columns (default: true)

check_row_counts.description: Check inserted row counts when execution metadata is available (default: true)

tools.name: infer_seed_rules
tools.description: 🌱 ADVANCED SEEDER: Infers safe seed generator rules from schema metadata, sample row patterns, unique constraints, and optional ecommerce/POS/CRM domain presets without returning raw PII samples.

tables.description: Optional table subset to analyze; defaults to schema tables up to max_tables

domain.description: Optional domain preset for better business-like dummy data (default: auto)

sample_size.description: Number of sample rows per table used to infer ranges and enum-like choices (default: 25, max: 100)

max_tables.description: Maximum number of tables to analyze when tables is omitted (default: 50)

tools.name: seed_from_template
tools.description: 🌱 TEMPLATE SEEDER: Creates a plan-first FK-aware seed workflow from ecommerce, POS, or CRM templates. It detects matching tables, applies domain rules, and returns a seed plan for preview/execution.

include.description: Optional concrete table list to seed instead of auto-detected template matches

exclude.description: Optional table names to ignore during template matching

rows_per_table.description: Optional row-count override for the generated plan

include_dependencies.description: Include parent tables required by foreign keys (default: true)

include_children.description: Include child tables that reference included template tables (default: false)

respect_existing_data.description: Reuse existing parent records when possible instead of creating new parent rows (default: true)

random_seed.description: Deterministic seed for repeatable preview and execution

require_confirmation.description: Require confirm_token for non-dry-run execution (default: true)

tools.name: run_select_query
tools.description: ⚡ PRIMARY TOOL FOR SELECT QUERIES. Executes read-only SELECT statements with parameterization, optimizer hints, query caching, and dry-run mode. Supports complex queries with JOINs, subqueries, and aggregations. ⚠️ ONLY for SELECT - use execute_write_query for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, use execute_ddl for CREATE/ALTER/DROP.

params.description: Optional array of parameters for parameterized queries

hints.description: Optional MySQL optimizer hints to apply to the query

straightJoin.description: Use STRAIGHT_JOIN to force join order

noCache.description: Disable query cache for this query

useCache.description: Whether to use query result caching (default: true)

dry_run.description: If true, returns query plan and estimated cost without executing (Safe Mode)

tools.name: execute_write_query
tools.description: ⚡ PRIMARY TOOL FOR INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE QUERIES. Executes data modification statements with parameterization support. Returns affected row count and execution details. ⚠️ NOT for SELECT (use run_select_query), NOT for DDL (use execute_ddl for CREATE/ALTER/DROP/TRUNCATE/RENAME).

query.description: SQL query to execute (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DDL if permitted)

params.description: Optional array of parameters for parameterized queries

tools.name: repair_query
tools.description: 🔧 Diagnoses SQL query errors and suggests fixes. Analyzes syntax errors, missing columns/tables, and logic issues. Provide the query and optional error message to get repair recommendations. Use when a query fails or needs debugging.

query.description: The SQL query to analyze or repair

error_message.description: Optional error message received when executing the query

tools.name: create_table
tools.description: 🏗️ Creates a new table with columns, data types, constraints, and indexes. Simplified interface compared to raw DDL. Requires "ddl" permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table to create

type.description: MySQL data type (e.g., VARCHAR(255), INT, TEXT)

primary_key.description: Whether this is the primary key

tools.name: alter_table
tools.description: 🔧 Modifies existing table structure: add/drop/modify/rename columns, add/drop indexes. Supports multiple operations in one call. Requires "ddl" permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table to alter

operations.description: Array of alter operations to perform

tools.name: drop_table
tools.description: 🗑️ DESTRUCTIVE: Permanently deletes a table and ALL its data. Cannot be undone! Requires "ddl" permission. ⚠️ WARNING: IRREVERSIBLE!

table_name.description: Name of the table to drop

if_exists.description: If true, will not error if table does not exist

tools.name: execute_ddl
tools.description: ⚡ PRIMARY TOOL FOR DDL STATEMENTS. Executes schema modification queries: CREATE, ALTER, DROP, TRUNCATE, RENAME. Use for complex DDL that structured tools don't cover. ⚠️ NOT for SELECT (use run_select_query), NOT for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (use execute_write_query). Requires "ddl" permission.

query.description: DDL SQL query to execute (must start with CREATE, ALTER, DROP, TRUNCATE, or RENAME - NO SELECT queries!)

tools.name: describe_connection
tools.description: Returns current database connection details: host, database name, user, port, and connection status. Use to verify which database you're connected to.

tools.name: cursor_execute_request
tools.description: Cursor compatibility bridge for clients that can call MCP tools but cannot send arguments. Reads .cursor/mysql-mcp-request.json (or MYSQL_MCP_CURSOR_REQUEST_FILE) and dispatches to the requested MySQL MCP tool. The request file supports {"tool":"execute_ddl","arguments":{"query":"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS t;"}} or direct SQL with {"query":"...","mode":"auto"}.

tools.name: read_changelog
tools.description: Reads the MySQL MCP Server changelog to see version history, new features, bug fixes, and breaking changes. Useful for understanding tool capabilities and recent updates.

tools.name: test_connection
tools.description: Tests database connectivity and measures latency. Returns connection status and response time. Use to troubleshoot connection issues or check database availability.

tools.name: list_all_tools
tools.description: 📋 Returns complete catalog of all available tools with names, descriptions, parameters, and current permissions. Use to discover tool capabilities or when users ask 'what can you do?'.

tools.name: get_all_tables_relationships
tools.description: Gets ALL table foreign key relationships in the entire database in a single efficient query. Returns a comprehensive relationship map showing parent-child connections between all tables. Much faster than querying table-by-table.

tools.name: begin_transaction
tools.description: 🔄 Starts a new database transaction and returns a transaction ID. Use with commit_transaction or rollback_transaction to group multiple operations atomically. Essential for data consistency when multiple changes must succeed or fail together.

transactionId.description: Optional custom transaction ID. If not provided, one will be generated.

tools.name: commit_transaction
tools.description: ✅ Commits an active transaction, making all changes permanent. Use after successful completion of all operations within a transaction started by begin_transaction.

tools.name: rollback_transaction
tools.description: ↩️ Rolls back an active transaction, undoing ALL changes made within it. Use when an error occurs during transaction or when changes need to be cancelled.

tools.name: get_transaction_status
tools.description: Shows all active transactions with their IDs, start times, and operation counts. Use to monitor transaction state or debug transaction issues.

tools.name: execute_in_transaction
tools.description: Executes a SQL query within an existing transaction context. The query becomes part of the transaction and will be committed or rolled back with it. Use after begin_transaction.

transactionId.description: The transaction ID to execute the query within

query.description: SQL query to execute within the transaction

params.description: Optional array of parameters for parameterized queries

tools.name: list_stored_procedures
tools.description: Lists all stored procedures in the specified database.

database.description: Optional: specific database name to list procedures from

tools.name: get_stored_procedure_info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific stored procedure including parameters and metadata.

procedure_name.description: Name of the stored procedure to get information for

tools.name: execute_stored_procedure
tools.description: Executes a stored procedure with optional parameters.

procedure_name.description: Name of the stored procedure to execute

parameters.description: Optional array of parameters to pass to the stored procedure

tools.name: create_stored_procedure
tools.description: Creates a new stored procedure with the specified parameters and body.

procedure_name.description: Name of the stored procedure to create

body.description: SQL body of the stored procedure

comment.description: Optional comment for the stored procedure

tools.name: drop_stored_procedure
tools.description: Drops (deletes) a stored procedure. WARNING: This is irreversible!

procedure_name.description: Name of the stored procedure to drop

if_exists.description: If true, will not error if procedure does not exist

tools.name: show_create_procedure
tools.description: Shows the CREATE statement for a stored procedure.

procedure_name.description: Name of the stored procedure to show CREATE statement for

tools.name: export_table_to_csv
tools.description: 📄 Exports table data to CSV format with filtering, pagination, and sorting. For sensitive data, use safe_export_table instead which includes automatic data masking.

table_name.description: Name of the table to export

value.description: Value to compare against (can be string, number, boolean, or array for "in" operator)

include_headers.description: Whether to include column headers in the CSV output

tools.name: export_query_to_csv
tools.description: 📄 Executes a SELECT query and exports results to CSV format. Supports complex queries with JOINs and aggregations. For sensitive data, consider using safe_export_table or adding manual data masking.

query.description: SQL SELECT query to execute and export

params.description: Optional array of parameters for parameterized queries

include_headers.description: Whether to include column headers in the CSV output

tools.name: analyze_query
tools.description: 🔍 Analyzes a SQL query using EXPLAIN and provides optimization suggestions: missing indexes, inefficient operations, cost estimates. Returns actionable recommendations. Use before running expensive queries.

tools.name: get_optimization_hints
tools.description: Get suggested MySQL optimizer hints for a specific optimization goal (SPEED, MEMORY, or STABILITY).

goal.description: Optimization goal: SPEED for faster queries, MEMORY for lower memory usage, STABILITY for consistent performance

tools.name: list_views
tools.description: Lists all views in the connected database.

tools.name: get_view_info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific view including columns and definition.

tools.name: create_view
tools.description: Creates a new view with the specified SELECT definition. Requires 'ddl' permission.

view_name.description: Name of the view to create

definition.description: SELECT statement that defines the view

tools.name: alter_view
tools.description: Alters an existing view definition. Requires 'ddl' permission.

view_name.description: Name of the view to alter

definition.description: New SELECT statement that defines the view

tools.name: drop_view
tools.description: Drops a view. Requires 'ddl' permission. WARNING: This is irreversible!

view_name.description: Name of the view to drop

if_exists.description: If true, will not error if view does not exist

tools.name: show_create_view
tools.description: Shows the CREATE statement for a view.

tools.name: list_triggers
tools.description: Lists all triggers in the database, optionally filtered by table.

table_name.description: Optional: filter triggers for specific table

tools.name: get_trigger_info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific trigger.

tools.name: create_trigger
tools.description: Creates a new trigger on a table. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Table the trigger is associated with

definer.description: Optional: user who owns the trigger

tools.name: drop_trigger
tools.description: Drops a trigger. Requires 'ddl' permission. WARNING: This is irreversible!

trigger_name.description: Name of the trigger to drop

if_exists.description: If true, will not error if trigger does not exist

tools.name: show_create_trigger
tools.description: Shows the CREATE statement for a trigger.

tools.name: list_indexes
tools.description: Lists all indexes for a specific table.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: get_index_info
tools.description: Gets detailed information about a specific index.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: create_index
tools.description: Creates a new index on a table. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

columns.description: Columns to index (string or object with column, length, order)

tools.name: drop_index
tools.description: Drops an index from a table. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

index_name.description: Name of the index to drop

tools.name: analyze_index
tools.description: Analyzes a table to update index statistics.

table_name.description: Name of the table to analyze

tools.name: list_foreign_keys
tools.description: Lists all foreign key constraints for a table.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: list_constraints
tools.description: Lists all constraints (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK) for a table.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: add_foreign_key
tools.description: Adds a foreign key constraint to a table. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: drop_foreign_key
tools.description: Drops a foreign key constraint. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

constraint_name.description: Name of the constraint to drop

tools.name: add_unique_constraint
tools.description: Adds a unique constraint to a table. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: drop_constraint
tools.description: Drops a UNIQUE or CHECK constraint. Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: add_check_constraint
tools.description: Adds a CHECK constraint to a table (MySQL 8.0.16+). Requires 'ddl' permission.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: analyze_table
tools.description: Analyzes a table to update index statistics for the query optimizer.

table_name.description: Name of the table to analyze

tools.name: optimize_table
tools.description: Optimizes a table to reclaim unused space and defragment data.

table_name.description: Name of the table to optimize

tools.name: check_table
tools.description: Checks a table for errors.

table_name.description: Name of the table to check

tools.name: repair_table
tools.description: Repairs a corrupted table (MyISAM, ARCHIVE, CSV only).

table_name.description: Name of the table to repair

tools.name: truncate_table
tools.description: 🗑️ DESTRUCTIVE: Removes ALL rows from a table instantly (faster than DELETE). Resets auto-increment counters. Cannot be undone! Requires 'ddl' permission. ⚠️ WARNING: IRREVERSIBLE!

table_name.description: Name of the table to truncate

tools.name: get_table_status
tools.description: Gets detailed status and statistics for one or all tables.

table_name.description: Optional: specific table name (omit for all tables)

tools.name: flush_table
tools.description: Flushes table(s) - closes and reopens them.

table_name.description: Optional: specific table (omit for all tables)

tools.name: get_table_size
tools.description: Gets size information for one or all tables including data and index sizes.

table_name.description: Optional: specific table name (omit for all tables)

tools.name: create_fulltext_index
tools.description: Creates a FULLTEXT index on one or more text columns in a table. Supports ngram and mecab parsers for advanced text search capabilities (useful for languages like Chinese, Japanese, Korean). Use this to enable full-text search on text columns.

table_name.description: Name of the table to create the fulltext index on

columns.description: Array of column names to include in the fulltext index

index_name.description: Optional: custom name for the index (default: auto-generated)

parser.description: Optional: parser for CJK languages (ngram or mecab)

ngram_token_size.description: Optional: token size for ngram parser (default: 2)

tools.name: fulltext_search
tools.description: Performs full-text search on a table using MATCH AGAINST clause. Supports natural language, boolean mode, and query expansion. Returns results ordered by relevance score. Use this for efficient text search on indexed columns.

table_name.description: Name of the table to search

columns.description: Optional: array of column names to search (default: all fulltext indexed columns)

order_by.description: Optional: column to order by (default: relevance_score)

tools.name: get_fulltext_info
tools.description: Retrieves information about FULLTEXT indexes on a table including index names, columns, and parser details. Use this to discover what fulltext indexes exist on a table.

table_name.description: Name of the table

index_name.description: Optional: specific index name to query (default: all indexes)

tools.name: drop_fulltext_index
tools.description: Drops a FULLTEXT index from a table. Use this to remove a fulltext index that is no longer needed.

table_name.description: Name of the table

index_name.description: Optional: name of the index to drop (default: first fulltext index found)

tools.name: get_fulltext_stats
tools.description: Retrieves statistics for FULLTEXT indexes on a table including document count, size, and key length. Use this to analyze index performance and size.

table_name.description: Name of the table

tools.name: optimize_fulltext
tools.description: Optimizes a table to update FULLTEXT index statistics and reclaim space. Use this after bulk inserts or updates to improve search performance.

table_name.description: Name of the table to optimize
